howItWorks.intro: This appliance energy cost calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Energy costs and incentives change by region and season. Check with your local utility for current rates.

faq.question: What appliances use the most electricity?
faq.answer: HVAC (heating/cooling): 40-50% of bills. Water heater: 15-20%. Washer/dryer: 10%. Refrigerator: 5-8%. Oven/stove: 3-5%. LED lights are minimal. Space heaters are expensive per-hour (1,500W).

faq.question: How many watts does a refrigerator use?
faq.answer: Modern refrigerators use 100-400W while running, but cycle on and off. Average annual usage is 400-600 kWh/year ($55-85/year). Older models can use 2-3× more.

faq.question: How do I find my appliance's wattage?
faq.answer: Check the label or plate on the back/bottom of the appliance. It lists wattage (W) or amps (A) × volts (V) = watts. You can also use a Kill-A-Watt meter plugged into the outlet for real-time measurement.
